title: SDF warning/error enhancement proposal
body:
I understand SDF support is limited and there is a GSOC project open as well. I came across a (very) low-hanging fruit type of enhancement and I have a local patch. Want to know if you are interested in a PR.

In my case, the DUT instance to be annotated is specified only inside SDF file, not during $sdf_annotate call (in Verilog). Now, when the instance name (or module name) is misspelled inside SDF I get:

```log
SDF WARNING: ../vlog_delay_mode_sdf.sv:42: Unable to find inst1 in scope dut_inv_with_delays. 
```
That line 42 in Verilog looks as follows:
```verilog
    $sdf_annotate("sample.sdf");  //, inst);
```
Here vvp found an instance named inst1 and that source is actually SDF, relevant line below:

```verilog
  (INSTANCE inst1)
```
So, a first-timer would be lost as to where the fix shall be. I enhanced it to pass SDF line number from sdf_parse.y to sys_sdf.c and have the below:

```log
SDF WARNING: ../vlog_delay_mode_sdf.sv:42: Unable to find inst1 in scope dut_inv_with_delays. SDF: sample.sdf 5
```
It is no rocket science, but does improve the usability IMHO. Also it is not perfect as it prints the line-number of CELL than the INSTANCE, but nevertheless it is showing the SDF file name and a close line. 

I do intend to enhance few more such low-hanging stuff around this area if this is of interest.

Thanks
